text:
There once was a young hare named Benny. Benny lived in a forest and was notorious amongst his animal friends for his exceptional speed. One morning while he was bouncing around the woods, gathering berries, he heard the most remarkable sound. It was like a bird humming a tune, but much more cheerful. Benny paused listening, mesmerized for what felt like minutes.

Who could that be? Benny wondered. Curious, he bounded over to where the sound was coming from. There was a hedgehog perched on a low branch, seemingly enjoying nothing more than humming its cheerful melody. Benny hadn't ever met a hedgehog who could do that; it was almost musical. Curious, Benny leaned closer.

The hedgehog looked up with large, wise eyes and giggled heartily. It wasn't cheerful music. It was counting sheep. Hard. And fast.

Benny laughed. "What a job!"

The hedgehog sighed dramatically, fluffing up its spines. "It seems impossible
